DRIVING DOWN THE ROAD THE ENTIRE CAR STARTED VIBRATING AND FELT WOBBLY WHEN I GOT ABOVE 20MPH. PULLED OVER TO CHECK TIRES AND UNDER THE CAR, NO OBVIOUS ISSUES FOUND. TURNED OFF THEN BACK ON THE CAR, SAME ISSUE ABOVE 20MPH. THE CAR WAS STRUGGLING TO GET UP THE TOW RAMP AND SMELLED

I PURCHASED MY ASCENT ON NOVEMBER 29, 2019.  BY DECEMBER 8, I HAD TWO CRACKS IN THE WINDSHIELD.  I LIVE IN A SMALL TOWN, AND WAS DRIVING LESS THAN 45MPH WHEN BOTH OF THESE CHIPS OCCURRED.  ONE WAS (ACCORDING TO SAFELITE) TOO SMALL TO REPAIR.   THE OTHER ONE WAS REPAIRED ON 12/10.

WE ARE ON OUR SECOND SUBARU ASCENT, FIRST ONE BOUGHT BACK FOR THE SAME ISSUE.  UPON STARTING THE CAR, THE CABIN WILL FILL WITH A STRONG SMELL OF RAW FUEL (NOT EXHAUST).  THE CAR WILL ALSO FILL WITH THE SMELL IF RUNNING WHILE PARKED, REMOTELY STARTED, ETC.  THE SMELL IS ALSO NOTIC

narrative.headline: 2020 Subaru Ascent: the electrical system tops the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 316 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 32%, followed by the power train (17%) and the windshield and wipers (16%). No deaths are reported. The brakes draw the fewest complaints of these areas but the most crashes (2) and injuries (2), so they are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 101 reports (32%), including 1 with fire. No crashes or injuries reported in this area.

issues.sample: DRIVING DOWN THE ROAD THE ENTIRE CAR STARTED VIBRATING AND FELT WOBBLY WHEN I GOT ABOVE 20MPH. PULLED OVER TO CHECK TIRES AND UNDER THE CAR, NO OBVIOUS ISSUES FOUND. TURNED OFF THEN BACK ON THE CAR, SAME ISSUE ABOVE 20MPH. THE CAR WAS STRUGGLING TO GET UP THE TOW RAMP AND SMELLED
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 55 reports (17%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries logged. Watch for vibration or wobble at speed on a test drive.

issues.sample: I PURCHASED MY ASCENT ON NOVEMBER 29, 2019.  BY DECEMBER 8, I HAD TWO CRACKS IN THE WINDSHIELD.  I LIVE IN A SMALL TOWN, AND WAS DRIVING LESS THAN 45MPH WHEN BOTH OF THESE CHIPS OCCURRED.  ONE WAS (ACCORDING TO SAFELITE) TOO SMALL TO REPAIR.   THE OTHER ONE WAS REPAIRED ON 12/10.
issues.title: Visibility/Wiper
issues.blurb: 49 reports (16%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners commonly mention windshield cracks and chips.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 29 reports (9%), tied to 1 crash, with no fires or injuries. A smaller but recurring complaint area.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The fewest of these areas at 26 reports (8%), but the most serious by outcome: 2 crashes and 2 injuries. Test the brakes thoroughly.
